title: Departing China — that's a wrap to this chapter
html:
TL;DR

Written on the flight out of china, Dec 14 2024. Yahir is making slides of his story and the slides "don't seem real." Frames the moment as the end of a meaningful, transformative chapter (china chapter) and the start of a new prolific life. Includes a legacy question-style line: "If I were to die today, my life will have been amazing." Closes: "That's a wrap to this chapter…"

Key quotes

"I am missed, I will miss, I am so fucking sad to leave behind this chapter of my life, but I'm sad because it was meaningful."

"There was beauty in the solitude, beauty in the adventure, beauty in the unknown, uncomfortable, and in the transformation of a new normal."

"Be so truly yourself it catches people off guard. Honest, genuine, and Yahir Gonzalez."

"We are sent here by God, and walk with Gods eyes."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Definitive specimen of end of chapter reflection — Yahir's transition-marker form.
  • The "if I died today" framing is now a tracked instance of the legacy question template (first set up in 2019 08 12 keep things simple).
  • Anchors the china chapter concept and motivates the gap between Dec 2024 (leaves China) and Sep 2025 (arrives austin tx per Memory).
  • "Be so truly yourself it catches people off guard" — first cleanly stated identity-as-strategy line; check for echoes in rankett meetup material.

Open questions

  • Where did he go between leaving China (Dec 2024) and arriving Austin (Sep 2025)? Likely grants pass or — confirm.
  • Are the "slides" he's making in this note a deck about the China experience? If so, are they archived?
  • Who are the "unforgettable" people from China that get -treatment vs explicit naming?

Wikilinks

china · china chapter · end of chapter reflection · legacy question · austin tx · grants pass or · Memory
